premise: In the neon-lit waterways beneath the city, underground surfers design and build their own hydro-boards to ride the storm drains. Across one season, two rivals — Circuit, a gadget-obsessed monkey engineer, and Riptide, a fearless cat daredevil — trade wins, defeats, and new strategies in a season-long duel that builds toward the legendary Sewer Crown race.
style_anchor: clean, simple 3D cartoon style like the modern Super Mario Bros. Movie and Nintendo/Illumination games: smooth simplified surfaces, glossy toy-like vinyl shading, bold simple shapes, strongly exaggerated NON-realistic proportions — a big oversized rounded head about one-third of total body height, very large round expressive eyes taking up much of the face, small rounded body, short stubby limbs, simplified appealing features. Minimal surface texture — fur and skin read as smooth, simple, flat-shaded cartoon forms, NOT individual hair strands, pores, or realistic detail. Bright saturated candy colors, soft clean cinematic lighting with gentle ambient occlusion and glossy wet highlights, shallow depth of field. Deliberately non-realistic, polished video-game-cartoon look — never photorealistic, never realistic fur, skin, or anatomy. Clothes are not detailed in terms of design, rely on silhouette

characters.name: circuit
characters.appearance_tokens: a small wiry monkey with warm golden-brown fur, an oversized rounded head with huge curious amber eyes, and a long prehensile tail; brainy gadgeteer in cool-toned beach streetwear — an oversized cyan-and-white mesh-panel windbreaker worn open, a baggy graphic tee, roomy cargo board-shorts, and chunky neon-yellow pool slides; a snorkel-and-goggle set pushed up on his head and a clear waterproof gadget sling-bag, signature yellow number 3
characters.equipment_tokens: a sensor-studded cyan hydro-surfboard bristling with small fins
characters.personality: brainy, calculating, over-prepared; wins by out-engineering the course with gadgets, telemetry, and clever lines

characters.name: riptide
characters.appearance_tokens: a lean, athletic cat with sleek charcoal-black fur, an oversized head with huge sharp green eyes, a torn ear and a spiky fur mohawk; fearless daredevil in bold warm-toned beach streetwear — a fitted sleeveless magenta-and-black graphic rashguard tank under an open palm-print bomber, long flame-and-wave print board-shorts, a shark-tooth cord necklace and grippy neon-orange reef shoes, signature white number 7
characters.equipment_tokens: a stripped-down minimalist neon-orange hydro-surfboard built for raw speed
characters.personality: reckless, fearless, rides on pure instinct and adrenaline; takes insane lines no sane surfer would attempt

active_threads: Circuit and Riptide are the scene's two top riders; the season-long duel for the Sewer Crown is just beginning
plot_state.last_cliffhanger: He vanished into a tunnel that isn't on any map — leaving their own mark glowing on the wall, daring them to follow.
episode_log: sewer-surfers_ep_0007: The Mark in the Board

world_anchor: Set in a vast neon-lit underground world beneath a rain-soaked city — storm drains, flood tunnels, sluice gates, graffiti-tagged concrete, glowing algae, and shafts of streetlight through the grates above; plus the riders' own lived-in spaces down there (cluttered board-building dens and workshops, hangouts, crew haunts). Any vehicle/board that appears is ONLY a custom water hydro-surfboard — never motorcycles, bikes, skateboards, wheels, handlebars, metal grind-rails, train tracks, or cars.

riding.anchor: The characters are actively SURFING right now: balanced (standing or crouched) on their custom finned hydro-surfboards, riding fast-rushing neon water down the tunnels with spray flying — boards clearly under their feet and rushing water beneath them, mid-action and fully committed to the ride, never idle or posed.
riding.description: actively surfing/riding their hydro-boards on the rushing water

grounded.anchor: The characters are on foot on solid ground — standing, walking, sitting, crouching, leaning, or gesturing on a solid surface (concrete walkway, floor, ledge, workshop floor). They are NOT surfing in this shot: no board under their feet and no rushing water beneath them — the ground is solid, dry footing. Any board present is an inert object being built, carried, or leaning, never ridden.
grounded.description: on foot on solid ground — dialogue, building/tinkering, reacting, walking (NOT riding)

logline: Two rival riders build custom hydro-boards and battle across a season for the Sewer Crown.
setting: A vast underground world of storm drains, flood tunnels, and sluice gates beneath a rain-soaked city. Rushing water, glowing algae, graffiti-tagged concrete, and shafts of streetlight through the grates above.
episode_format: Each episode is a ~60-75s self-contained beat that ALSO advances the season arc. Always: a frame-1 hook, a clear stakes setup, the Circuit-vs-Riptide contrast (gadgets vs. raw nerve) where relevant, and a loopable cliffhanger that pulls toward the next episode and the looming Sewer Crown. NOT every episode is a race — vary the purpose per `arc.episode_purposes` (a new strategy, a defeat, a character or relationship beat, a training run, rising buildup) so races feel like special, anticipated events.
tone: high-energy Pixar-style sports rivalry with comedic beats and real emotional stakes

arc.synopsis: A 20-episode season that uses the Sewer Crown rivalry as the SPINE for a character-driven story — not a string of races. Circuit and Riptide begin as clashing rivals (cold engineering vs. raw instinct), and across the season their relationship visibly evolves: friction, a grudging respect, an uneasy alliance, a falling-out, and a hard-won bond. Between the handful of tentpole races, episodes build out the underground surf world — its crews and characters, the graffiti-tagged tunnels, the lore and origin of the Sewer Crown, why each rider really rides — and develop the two leads as people. Races are rare, anticipated events that punctuate the arc rather than define it; the real throughline is the rivalry maturing into something deeper as it escalates toward the Crown.
arc.finale: Episode 20 IS the Sewer Crown race — the season's biggest event — but its true payoff is the Circuit–Riptide relationship landing where the season has grown it (rivals turned partners/true friends). The winner is revealed and it closes on a victory lap that resolves their bond, not just the scoreboard.
episode_purposes: a high-stakes race or qualifying heat (RARE — a special, spaced-out tentpole event, not the default)
inventing, building, or testing a new board, gadget, or strategy
a defeat or setback that forces a rethink and reveals character
a character-development or backstory beat (who they are, why they ride, what they fear)
deepening, testing, fracturing, or repairing the Circuit-Riptide relationship
world-building & lore: the underground surf scene, its crews and culture, the history and legend of the Sewer Crown
a training run or risky trial, or a rival crew / external threat to the scene
rising-tension buildup and stakes-raising toward an upcoming race
arc.pacing_notes: Open the season (ep 1) on a high-stakes race to hook viewers. After that, races are RARE tentpoles — only ~4-5 of the 20 episodes are actual races/heats (roughly ep 1, ~6, ~12, ~16, and the finale 20). The MAJORITY of episodes are non-race: building/strategy, defeats, character & backstory, relationship beats, and world-building/lore — every one must move the story or the rivalry forward (no filler or aimless downtime). Treat the Circuit–Riptide relationship as the season's real arc and let it move episode to episode (rivals → friction → respect → uneasy alliance → falling-out → bond) so viewers return for the STORY, not just the next race. Every episode still plants or pays off a seed for the Sewer Crown finale; as the number approaches 20, raise the stakes, and episode 20 delivers the Crown race, the winner, and a relationship-resolving victory lap.
